title: [BUG] Touch events conflict with Element Plus el-slider on mobile devices (Slider becomes unresponsive)
author: zhanghaifeng213
body:
Hi team,
I encountered an issue where the el-slider component from Element Plus becomes unresponsive (cannot slide) when it is placed inside a container managed by panzoom on mobile devices.
It seems that panzoom's touch event handlers are intercepting or conflicting with the slider's native touch events, preventing the user from changing the value.

Reproduction Steps:
1. Create a page with a panzoom instance wrapping a content area.
2. Inside the panzoom content, place an Element Plus <el-slider> component.
3. Open the page on a mobile device (or use Chrome DevTools Mobile Emulation).
4. Attempt to drag the slider handle.

Expected Behavior:
The slider should respond to touch drag events and change its value normally, without triggering the pan/zoom action of the parent container.

Actual Behavior:
The slider handle does not move. Instead, the parent container might pan, or nothing happens at all. The slider is effectively "frozen".

title: Get coordinates when clicking on panzoom ?
author: FomaFomitch
body:
Hello,

Events works great thanks you for this library. But I wonder, is it possible to know WHERE the cursor is clicked on the map ? Because we apply transformation from the direction of the click, but how to get where the cursor is, relative to panzoom element ?

title: panend event should be fired when the animated panning ends
author: ivanjx
body: currently, `panend` event is fired on mouse up (released). the actual panning might not be done yet if `smoothScroll` is `true`. this is important for me since i need to save the pan and zoom state efficiently and accurately.

title: BUG: Sudden zoom when tapping on larger pinch lengths
author: Jasonvdlee
body:
Hi,

There is an issue when you zoom using pinch gestures. When you tap/press the second finger down on your screen while using a "larger" pinch length between the two fingers, then you instantly zoom in based on the length of the fingers. I tried to look for a work-around, but can't get something else to work.

**Expected behaviour**
The moment the second finger lands on the screen, nothing should happen. As soon as you make pinching gestures from there, the container should zoom in/out.

**Actual behaviour**
The moment the second finger lands, it already calculates the zoom and instantly applies it.

Thank you!

title: Bounds not working
author: Lamerchun

title: Pan to element in container
author: Jasonvdlee
body:
Hello,

I simply want to utilize the `instance.moveTo(x, y)` function to center a specific element within the container of the screen. I have read that the x and y values depend on the current zoom level. Is that true? That would make things a little more difficult. I do have the position of the element within the container. I am just unsure what the panzoom library does exactly that could potentially affect the calculation I am making to determine the position of the element.

Tl;dr: I have an element in the panzoom container. How do I center it by panning to that position?

title: Panzoom breaks 'position: fixed' + can't make popups due to panning jumps
author: BrancuAlexandru
body: When putting a popup in the page, maybe the size of the body element changes but my panzoom jumps randomly. Also, I have an element which I want to set 'position: fixed' on. This simply doesn't work. I tried to apply panzoom to an element outside of the parent of the fixed element, but that doesn't fix it either. I don't know how to fix it and I haven't seen other issues about this while looking around.

title: zoomAbs, smoothZoom, moveTo etc weird effects
author: mainiomooses
body:
Using zoomAbs, smoothZoom, moveTo etc. results in unexpected behavior. It's hard to explain, so here's a video capture with pressing a button to trigger zoomAbs(0, 0, 1) on the instance.

The results range from nothing happening to the instance zooming/panning in/out in a manner I can't explain. Triggering smoothZoom, moveTo etc. result in nigh similar behavior.

Link to video (Youtube): https://youtu.be/dVrrnDt_HNA

title: Pan is too sensitive
author: VictorDementiev
body:
Hello there,

Maybe someone faced a case when quick clicks in the panned element move this element? (because there are micro-movements of the mouse)
Is there a way to reduce sensitivity of panning? 
For example, I don't want to pan the element in case if it will be moved to 4px or less.
